### Post by siddharth_univ on 2011-12-07
Hi Guys,

I just installed kubuntu 11.10 64 bit and i have a matrox triple head to go with a single nvidia 485m graphics card on a laptop. I get automatic detection of 3840*1024 in nvidia control panel. I used matrox triple head to go in windows and i get 5760*1080 resolution. How do i setup to get a resolution of 5760*1080?

Here is xorg.conf file:

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 290.10  (buildd@charichuelo)  Wed Nov 23 07:50:49 UTC 2011

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "HannStar Display Corp HSD173PUW1"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 75.0
    VertRefresh     60.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce GTX 485M"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder" "DFP-2"
    Option         "metamodes" "DFP-0: nvidia-auto-select +0+0, DFP-2: nvidia-auto-select +1920+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection



What modifications do i need to get this running?

thanks

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2011-12-08
What's in the /var/log/Xorg.0.log file ?

Your resolution looks to be 3x1280 x 1024 which is the vesa driver max..

Is your xorg.conf file generated from nvidia-xconfig ?

I have attached Xorg.0.log file. I checked if the /etc/X11/xorg.conf is the same when using kubuntu and now using ubuntu, and theres no difference.. ### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2011-12-08
Can you stick this into your xorg.conf file:
Option "ModeDebug" "True"

Then logout/login & repost the Xorg.0.log file..

Can set the mode in xorg.conf but better to not need to...

Your log file shows:
[    22.025] (II) NVIDIA(0): Virtual screen size determined to be 5760 x 1080

I notice that appearing with the latest driver is this warning about twinvieworder referring to CRT & TV..strange..

---

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2011-12-08
The nVidia driver readme is extremely vague about these details. A minor criticism considering the volume of info..

Just stick into ServerFlags section if you have one or in device..I don't think it matters as it is global setting for nVidia driver module not for Xorg.

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2011-12-08
It is very generous with its verbiage.

But nVidia linux twinview only supports (2) displays , unlike nView windows..It must know you are cheating & have to pay the quadro tax.

2x 1920 = 3840..

I think the driver will not allow a virtual twinview screen bigger than 3840x1080.

The matrox device EDID reports a max res of 3840x1024.
Maybe the window drivers has a patch to override this video mode..

The only multi-monitor >2 support in linux nVidia is mosaic in quadro cards.
Afraid AMD beats nVidia hands down with Eye-Finity.

So:
I would use (3) separate X screens &/or run (2) screens twinview on matrox & (1) screen direct to video card.
Or try (3) separate X screens.

Try to grab the working modeline from in windows with powerstrip or nVidia driver..
Could than force it in xorg.conf but twinview is (2) screens only.

---

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2011-12-08
Maybe that unity 11.10 is stopping separate X screens from working..
I don't know because my multi-monitor setup runs 10.04..

The nVidia (windows) driver will let you display the detail of current video modeline & tweak it (& save/apply etc).
The video mode format may not be immediately useable..but it should be easy to massage it into shape. I have not needed to resort to this before.

It is quite possible that windows or win driver has patches for this h/w that linux does not know.

This is a calculated modeline (CVT)
# 5760x1080 29.95 Hz (CVT) hsync: 33.00 kHz; pclk: 240.25 MHz
Modeline "5760x1080_30.00"  240.25  5760 5944 6520 7280  1080 1083 1093 1102 -hsync +vsync

Can not recall the windows driver details..powerstrip is a window modeline calculator, I think it can snoop the current screen video mode.

---

### Post by aa-hcl on 2011-12-10
Hi,

I just want to say about my experience on setting up matrox dualhead2go on my laptop with nvidia card. I guess the same also applied to triple headtogo. I actually tried matrox support, but they did not help much, so I had to work it out myself searching through forums and getting various ideas from different post.


1. You need the EDID information stored in DualHead2Go box can be printed in linux
to /var/log/Xorg.0.log file if 

  - either start X as " startx -- -verbose 6 -logverbose 6 "
  - or put in the /etc/X11/xorg,conf file the line in Monitor section:
        Option "ModeDebug" "TRUE"

2. With the above modifications nvidia driver prints all the EDID
information including the debuging modes. However, it is not enough! We
also need the following lines in the xorg.conf

    Option "ModeValidation" "CRT-0: NoEdidMaxPClkCheck, NoMaxPClkCheck,
NoExtendedGpuCapabilitiesCheck"

(without this option nvidia refuses to accept mode it reads from EDID!)

If you got your mode listed as supported,  in the log file (with MOdeDebuf True) you will find the parameters that are need to set it up.

My Xorg file:

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 270.41.06  (buildmeister@swio-display-x86-rhel47-08.nvidia.com)  Mon Apr 18 15:14:00 PDT 2011

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 270.29  (buildd@allspice)  Fri Feb 25 14:42:07 UTC 2011

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Modes"
    Identifier         "Monitor0"
    ModeLine     "2048x768_113" 113.785 2048 2136 2200 2352 768 771 786 806
    ModeLine     "2048x768_137" 137.812 2048 2120 2176 2296 768 769 784 800
    ModeLine     "2048x768_165" 165.375 2048 2160 2232 2408 768 769 781 808
    ModeLine     "2560x1024_168" 168.750 2560 2584 2616 2640 1024 1025 1037 1066
    ModeLine     "3840x1200_60" 386.75 3840 4104 4512 5184 1200 1203 1213 1245 -hsync +vsync
    ModeLine     "3840x1200_386.31" 386.31 3840 4096 4512 5184 1200 1201 1204 1242 -hsync +vsync
    Modeline     "3840x1200_60_0" 290.23 3840 3864 3896 3920 1200 1203 1217 1235 +hsync -vsync
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "MatroxDualHead2Go"
    HorizSync       31.5 - 80.0
    VertRefresh     60.0 - 75.0
    Modeline       "3840x1200_60_0" 290.23 3840 3864 3896 3920 1200 1203 1217 1235 +hsync -vsync
    ModeLine       "3840x1200_60" 386.75 3840 4104 4512 5184 1200 1203 1213 1245 -hsync +vsync
    Option         "DPMS"	
    Option "ModeDebug" "TRUE"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "NVS 4200M"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder" "CRT-0"
    Option         "metamodes" "CRT: 3840x1200_60 +1920+0, DFP: nvidia-auto-select +0+0"
    Option "ModeValidation" "CRT-0: NoEdidMaxPClkCheck, NoMaxPClkCheck, NoExtendedGpuCapabilitiesCheck"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "3840x1200"
    EndSubSection
EndSection


I guess you do the same with triple head to go.

---
